summary: Checkout payments were unavailable for customers in the US region for 42 minutes on Saturday morning.

severity.rationale: Customer-facing outage or complete inability to use a critical flow.

evidence.text: Checkout payments were unavailable for customers in the US region for 42 minutes on Saturday morning.
Customers in the US region could not complete card payments during the incident. Approximately 18% of checkout attempts failed.

contributing_factors.description: A deployment changed the payment gateway connection pool limit from 100 to 20.

evidence.text: A deployment changed the payment gateway connection pool limit from 100 to 20. The lower limit exhausted available connections during normal Saturday traffic and caused payment authorization requests to fail.

contributing_factors.description: The lower limit exhausted available connections during normal Saturday traffic and caused payment authorization requests to fail.

evidence.text: A deployment changed the payment gateway connection pool limit from 100 to 20. The lower limit exhausted available connections during normal Saturday traffic and caused payment authorization requests to fail.

evidence.text: Add a pre-deploy configuration check for payment gateway pool limits.

A deployment changed the payment gateway connection pool limit from 100 to 20. The lower limit exhausted available connections during normal Saturday traffic and caused payment authorization requests to fail.

action_items.description: Add an alert for connection pool saturation before customer-facing failures occur.

evidence.text: Add an alert for connection pool saturation before customer-facing failures occur.

A deployment changed the payment gateway connection pool limit from 100 to 20. The lower limit exhausted available connections during normal Saturday traffic and caused payment authorization requests to fail.
Customers in the US region could not complete card payments during the incident. Approximately 18% of checkout attempts failed.

action_items.description: Document the rollback procedure for payment gateway configuration changes.

evidence.text: Document the rollback procedure for payment gateway configuration changes.

A deployment changed the payment gateway connection pool limit from 100 to 20. The lower limit exhausted available connections during normal Saturday traffic and caused payment authorization requests to fail.

supporting_evidence.text: Customers in the US region could not complete card payments during the incident. Approximately 18% of checkout attempts failed.

A deployment changed the payment gateway connection pool limit from 100 to 20. The lower limit exhausted available connections during normal Saturday traffic and caused payment authorization requests to fail.
The team rolled back the connection pool configuration and verified payment authorization success rates recovered.

09:02 UTC - Alert fired for elevated payment authorization failures. | 09:07 UTC - On-call engineer confirmed checkout payment attempts were failing. | 09:19 UTC - Payment gateway connection pool saturation identified. | 09:34 UTC - Connection pool configuration rolled back. | 09:44 UTC - Payment authorization success rate returned to normal.
